response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong polyglot engineer whose primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with only roughly six months of professional C++ work at National Instruments — far short of the 5–10 years mandated. He has no demonstrable capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain experience; his closest financial exposure is a fintech startup (RIDE Capital) focused on retail investment tooling in Germany, which does not satisfy the domain requirement. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, or any familiarity with capital-markets infrastructure such as Summit or messaging frameworks in a financial context.
strengths: Broad software engineering background spanning multiple languages and paradigms, showing strong adaptability
Solid CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab CI, Docker, Kubernetes, trunk-based development)
Demonstrated communication skills through conference talks, workshops, and open-source community involvement
concerns: C++ experience is approximately six months (NI LabWindows/CVI, 2014–2015) versus the 5–10 years required
No fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) is orthogonal to the role's Windows C++ and SQL Server/Oracle requirements
